sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Gregory, presents with a non-healing foot ulcer on his right foot, which has been present for approximately two months.
The ulcer has recently become red and is draining, causing concern for the patient.
The patient reports walking barefoot on the beach at Dewey Beach as the potential cause of the injury.
He denies any specific pain, but reports occasional throbbing.
The patient's biggest concern is the ulcer's redness and drainage.
He has not checked his blood sugar levels regularly and is unsure of his last A1C result, which he believes was around 10.
Past medical history: The patient has diabetes, which is not well-controlled, as evidenced by his high A1C result.
He has not had any recent treatment for his diabetes, and his PCP has not created a treatment plan for him.
Review of systems: The patient does not report any other symptoms, but the doctor notes that the patient's diabetes is not well-controlled.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs are normal, with no fever.
Physical exam: The patient has a one-by-two inch circular wound on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, proximal to the fifth MTP joint.
The wound is red, draining, and has some edema around it.
There is no necrosis, odor, or bony exposure.
The patient reports increased pressure when the wound is touched, but no distinct pain.
Test Results: The patient had an X-ray of his right foot, which showed no evidence of osteomyelitis.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a diabetic foot ulcer with an underlying infection.
Problem list: Diabetic foot ulcer; Infection; Poorly controlled diabetes.
Plan:
I prescribed clindamycin 400mg, taken twice a day for seven days, to treat the infection.
I will also perform a debridement to remove dead tissue.
The patient will need to wear a surgical shoe to reduce pressure on the foot.
I will order an ABI (ankle-brachial index) to determine the blood supply to the foot.
The patient will follow up in two weeks for another debridement and to assess the wound's progress.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen in two weeks for a follow-up appointment.
